summary: The review of "MTA Example 3.pdf," a Data Transfer Agreement, indicates a moderate risk posture overall. The primary concern is the governing law clause, which specifies Ontario, Canada, as the jurisdiction, deviating from the University of Auckland's preferred or acceptable positions. Additionally, the publication and termination clauses materially conflict with UoA's standards, requiring escalation for renegotiation. It is recommended to escalate the flagged clauses to General Counsel and the Senior Manager, Research Grants and Contracts for review and potential amendments to align with UoA's policies.

flags.clause_title: a) “Applicable Law” means all applicable laws, regulations, guidelines and polic
flags.snippet: a) “Applicable Law” means all applicable laws, regulations, guidelines and policies, including PHIPA and PIPEDA;
flags.rationale: The clause references PHIPA and PIPEDA, which are Canadian privacy laws. This suggests the governing law may be tied to Canada, which is not listed as either Preferred or Acceptable under UoA Position #POS-16 for Governing Law and Jurisdiction. The clause materially deviates from UoA's Preferred and Acceptable positions, requiring escalation to General Counsel for review and potential amendment.
flags.text: a) “Applicable Law” means all applicable laws, regulations, guidelines and policies, including PHIPA and PIPEDA;

flags.clause_title: 8. Publication. Receiving Party shall have the right to use a) the analyzed, de
flags.snippet: 8. Publication. Receiving Party shall have the right to use a) the analyzed, de-identified data derived from the use of the Data; and b) information and results arising out of analysis of the Data, as part of a publicat…
flags.rationale: The clause materially deviates from UoA Position #POS-12 on Publication. While it acknowledges the Disclosing Party's contribution and prohibits inclusion of personally identifying information, it imposes a requirement for prior written consent from the Disclosing Party before publication or presentation. This conflicts with UoA's Preferred Position, which grants the Principal Investigator the right to publish with only a reasonable patent stand-down (no more than 60 days). Additionally, the clause does not explicitly preserve students' publication rights for theses or academic publications, which is a critical UoA requirement. The clause must be escalated to the Senior Manager, Research Grants and Contracts for review and potential renegotiation.
flags.text:
8. Publication. Receiving Party shall have the right to use a) the analyzed, de-identified data derived from the
use of the Data; and b) information and results arising out of analysis of the Data, as part of a publication or
presentation of the results of the Study. The Receiving Party shall not include any personally identifying
information in any publication or presentation. Disclosing Party’s contribution to the Study shall be
acknowledged appropriately in any such publication or presentation in accordance with academic standards.
Prior written consent shall be obtained by the Disclosing Party before any such publication or presentation.
As applicable, Receiving Party agrees to share the Data and results arising from its analysis of the Data with
Disclosing Party, which Disclosing Party may use for academic research, teaching and scientific and
academic publications purposes. The Parties agree that raw level Data may be published in accordance with
funding or publication requirements.

flags.clause_title: i) Governing Law: This Agreement shall be governed, construed and interpreted
flags.snippet: i) Governing Law: This Agreement shall be governed, construed and interpreted pursuant to and in accordance with the laws of the Province of Ontario, and the laws of Canada applicable to the Province of Ontario. The provincial or federal courts having jurisdiction in Canada shall have exclusive jurisdiction over all matters pertaining to this Agreement.
flags.rationale: The clause specifies governing law as the Province of Ontario and Canada, which falls outside both the Preferred (New Zealand) and Acceptable tiers (Australia, the United Kingdom, Common European countries, Singapore, or non-exclusive jurisdiction of a specific US state). Exclusive jurisdiction in Canada further deviates from UoA's acceptable positions. This requires escalation to General Counsel for review and negotiation.
flags.text:
i) Governing Law: This Agreement shall be governed, construed and interpreted pursuant to and in
accordance with the laws of the Province of Ontario, and the laws of Canada applicable to the Province of
Ontario. The provincial or federal courts having jurisdiction in Canada shall have exclusive jurisdiction over
all matters pertaining to this Agreement.

flags.clause_title: l) Termination: The Parties may terminate this Agreement by providing thirty (
flags.snippet: l) Termination: The Parties may terminate this Agreement by providing thirty (30) days prior written notice to the other Parties.
flags.rationale: The clause allows termination for convenience by either party with 30 days' notice, which is outside the UoA Preferred Position for Termination (#POS-19). UoA prefers mutual two-way termination for material breach or insolvency, with clear rights for UoA to terminate for non-payment and provisions for termination costs. Termination for convenience is only acceptable for Consulting contracts under specific conditions, which are not met here. Escalation to the Senior Manager, Research Grants and Contracts is required.
flags.text:
l) Termination: The Parties may terminate this Agreement by providing thirty (30) days prior written notice
to the other Parties.

flags.clause_title: 3. Non-Disclosure of Data. The Receiving Party shall limit access to the Data
flags.snippet: 3. Non-Disclosure of Data. The Receiving Party shall limit access to the Data only to its Study Staff. The Receiving Party agrees that it/he/she shall, and shall require its/his/her Study Staff, to:
flags.rationale: The clause addresses confidentiality but does not explicitly align with UoA's Preferred Position #POS-11, which requires exclusions for public information, independently developed information, previously known information, and legally required disclosures. Additionally, UoA must retain the ability to disclose to agents and Affiliates, and confidentiality duration must be limited to 5 years post-completion. The clause does not specify these exclusions or the duration, and therefore falls into the Acceptable tier for confidentiality obligations limited in time (e.g., 5–7 years post-termination/expiry/disclosure). Escalation to the Senior Manager, Research Grants and Contracts is recommended for further review.
flags.text:
3. Non-Disclosure of Data. The Receiving Party shall limit access to the Data only to its Study Staff. The
Receiving Party agrees that it/he/she shall, and shall require its/his/her Study Staff, to:

flags.clause_title: 4. Safeguards and Notification. The Receiving Party shall use appropriate safe
flags.snippet: 4. Safeguards and Notification. The Receiving Party shall use appropriate safeguards, but no less stringent safeguards that Receiving Party employs to protect its own confidential and proprietary information (including …
flags.rationale: This clause addresses confidentiality obligations, which align partially with UoA Position #POS-11. While it includes safeguards for protecting confidential information and mentions obligations surviving termination, it does not explicitly exclude information already public, independently developed, previously known, or legally required to be disclosed. Additionally, it does not specify a time limit for confidentiality obligations (e.g., up to 5 years post-completion). These deviations place the clause in the Acceptable tier, requiring confirmation by the Contract Manager. Escalation to the Senior Manager, Research Grants and Contracts may be necessary if further alignment is required.
flags.text:
4. Safeguards and Notification. The Receiving Party shall use appropriate safeguards, but no less stringent
safeguards that Receiving Party employs to protect its own confidential and proprietary information
(including without limitation with respect to encrypting identifying numbers, linking files, storing and
retrieving files from secured locations) to prevent any unauthorized use or disclosure of the Data and shall
promptly report to Disclosing Party any unauthorized use or disclosure of which Receiving Party becomes
aware. Such safeguards and protections shall be used by Receiving Party for all Data received from
Disclosing Party. The above shall apply to any copies of the Data made by the Disclosing Party and kept at
their premises. All PHI transferred or exchanged under this Agreement shall be done so in accordance with
Schedule “B” herein. Receiving Party shall take appropriate care in the disposal or destruction of the
information to prevent unauthorized parties from gaining access to it. The Parties shall make their employees
and agents aware of the importance of maintaining the confidentiality of any collected or transferred PHI.
These obligations of confidentiality shall survive the expiration or earlier termination of this Agreement.

flags.clause_title: 6. Intellectual Property/Ownership. Except as expressly provided herein, no ri
flags.snippet: 6. Intellectual Property/Ownership. Except as expressly provided herein, no right, title or interest in and to the Data is granted to the Receiving Party or implied hereunder. The Receiving Party hereby specifically disclaims any right, title or interest of any kind whatsoever to the Data, results, and any discovery or invention arising out of the Study (as applicable). Patient medical records shall remain at all times the property of the Disclosing Party.
flags.rationale: The clause addresses Intellectual Property ownership and rights, which are explicitly outside the scope of UoA Contracting Positions and must be referred to Auckland UniServices Ltd for review. This aligns with UoA Position #POS-IP, which mandates referral of all IP clauses regardless of wording.
flags.text:
6. Intellectual Property/Ownership. Except as expressly provided herein, no right, title or interest in and to
the Data is granted to the Receiving Party or implied hereunder. The Receiving Party hereby specifically
disclaims any right, title or interest of any kind whatsoever to the Data, results, and any discovery or
invention arising out of the Study (as applicable). Patient medical records shall remain at all times the
property of the Disclosing Party.

flags.clause_title: n) “Study Staff” means internal personnel and/or agents of each Party who need
flags.snippet: n) “Study Staff” means internal personnel and/or agents of each Party who need access to the Data for the purposes herein and who are bound by the same confidentiality obligations herein.
flags.rationale: The clause touches on confidentiality obligations but does not explicitly align with UoA's Preferred Position #POS-11, which requires exclusions for public information, independently developed information, previously known information, and legally required disclosures. Additionally, the clause does not specify a duration for confidentiality obligations (e.g., up to 5 years post-completion). While it is acceptable for confidentiality obligations to apply to agents and personnel, the lack of clarity on exclusions and duration warrants an amber flag for further review by the Contract Manager.
flags.text: n) “Study Staff” means internal personnel and/or agents of each Party who need access to the Data for the purposes herein and who are bound by the same confidentiality obligations herein.

flags.clause_title: e) in the event that the Receiving Party retains a third party to house or main
flags.snippet: e) in the event that the Receiving Party retains a third party to house or maintain the database or otherwise transfers the Data to a third party, that Receiving Party will ensure that any such third party is contractually bound by obligations of patient confidentiality, privacy, security and non-use with respect to the Data no less stringent than those contained herein.
flags.rationale: The clause addresses confidentiality obligations when transferring data to third parties, which aligns with the UoA-sensitive topic of Confidentiality under Position #POS-11. However, it does not explicitly address the exclusions for public information, independently developed information, or previously known information, nor does it specify a duration for confidentiality obligations. These omissions place the clause in the Acceptable tier, requiring confirmation by the Contract Manager. Escalation to the Senior Manager, Research Grants and Contracts may be necessary if further clarification or adjustments are required.
flags.text:
e) in the event that the Receiving Party retains a third party to house or maintain the database or otherwise
transfers the Data to a third party, that Receiving Party will ensure that any such third party is
contractually bound by obligations of patient confidentiality, privacy, security and non-use with respect
to the Data no less stringent than those contained herein.

flags.clause_title: a) Term: This Agreement shall become effective as of the Effective Date and sh
flags.snippet: a) Term: This Agreement shall become effective as of the Effective Date and shall expire upon termination or upon earlier completion of the Study. The term of this Agreement may be amended by an Amendment Document, agre…
flags.rationale: The clause addresses the term and amendment process but does not explicitly align with UoA's Preferred Position on Termination (POS-19). While it mentions termination, it does not specify mutual two-way termination for material breach or insolvency, nor does it address UoA's right to terminate for non-payment or termination costs. The clause is acceptable for amendment processes but requires confirmation that termination provisions elsewhere in the contract align with UoA Positions. Escalation to Senior Manager Research Grants and Contracts may be required if termination provisions are unclear or non-compliant.
flags.text:
a) Term: This Agreement shall become effective as of the Effective Date and shall expire upon termination
or upon earlier completion of the Study. The term of this Agreement may be amended by an Amendment
Document, agreed to and signed by all Parties.

flags.clause_title: m) Dispute Resolution: The Parties shall make reasonable efforts to amicably r
flags.snippet: m) Dispute Resolution: The Parties shall make reasonable efforts to amicably resolve any issues, claims, disputes, controversy or inconsistencies involving this agreement or the protocol and to escalate the matter to se…
flags.rationale: The clause aligns partially with UoA Position #POS-18 on Dispute Resolution. It includes escalation to senior management and a commitment to amicable resolution, which matches the preferred tier. However, it omits mediation before arbitration/litigation, does not specify that proceedings must be in English, and does not explicitly preserve the right to urgent interlocutory court action. These omissions place the clause in the amber tier, as it deviates from the preferred wording but does not fall outside the acceptable tier. Escalation to the Senior Manager, Research Grants and Contracts or General Counsel is recommended for review.
flags.text:
m) Dispute Resolution: The Parties shall make reasonable efforts to amicably resolve any issues, claims,
disputes, controversy or inconsistencies involving this agreement or the protocol and to escalate the matter
to senior management prior to the commencement of any legal proceedings.

flags.clause_title: 1. Prevailing Terms. In case of a conflict between the terms of this Schedule “
flags.snippet: 1. Prevailing Terms. In case of a conflict between the terms of this Schedule “B” and any other terms of this Agreement, the terms of this Schedule “B” shall prevail. Any undefined terms shall have the same meaning as i…
flags.rationale: The clause addresses a topic (prevailing terms in case of conflict) that is not covered by the UoA Preferred Contracting Positions document. There is no specific policy guidance or template language for this type of clause, and it does not conflict with any existing UoA positions.
flags.text:
1. Prevailing Terms. In case of a conflict between the terms of this Schedule “B” and any other
terms of this Agreement, the terms of this Schedule “B” shall prevail. Any undefined terms shall have the
same meaning as in the Agreement.

flags.clause_title: 2. **Compliance.** The Disclosing Party wishes to share Data for the above-ref
flags.snippet: 2. **Compliance.** The Disclosing Party wishes to share Data for the above-referenced Study. In transferring the Data, the Parties shall comply with all Applicable Laws. The Disclosing Party represents that it has autho…
flags.rationale: The clause primarily addresses compliance with applicable laws, data transfer protocols, and audit rights, none of which are explicitly covered by the UoA Preferred Contracting Positions or the UoA Standard Template. While the comparator flagged Governing Law and Jurisdiction (POS-16), this clause does not specify governing law or jurisdiction, making the flag misplaced. The clause falls outside the scope of the Positions document and requires no escalation.
flags.standard_ref: Not covered by UoA Position or Template
flags.text:
2. **Compliance.** The Disclosing Party wishes to share Data for the above-referenced Study. In transferring the Data, the Parties shall comply with all Applicable Laws. The Disclosing Party represents that it has authorization to enter into and transfer the Data under Applicable Law. The Disclosing Party will prepare and furnish the Data in accordance with the Protocol including without limitation obtaining all applicable and appropriate consents. The parties hereto shall use a secure method of provision of the Data by Disclosing Party to Receiving Party in compliance with Applicable Laws. The Data will not be transferred until the Disclosing Party’s REB and, if applicable the Receiving Party’s REB, have approved the Protocol. The Disclosing Party retains the right to conduct audits of Receiving Party’s compliance with this Agreement upon reasonable advance written notice to Receiving Party and at mutually acceptable times. If there is a breach of the Agreement by Receiving Party, Receiving Party shall promptly provide notice to Disclosing Party. Disclosing Party may require that all Data be returned promptly to Disclosing Party or destroyed in a secure manner, at Disclosing Party’s option. The Disclosing Party retains the right, acting on reasonable grounds, to refuse the transfer of the Data requested hereunder. Where applicable, the Data will not be transferred until the Receiving Party provides evidence that the Receiving Party’s REB has approved the Protocol as attached in Schedule A.  

[Page 2]

flags.clause_title: 2. DISCLOSING PARTY subject to PHIPA. DISCLOSING PARTY is, as defined in PHIPA
flags.snippet: 2. DISCLOSING PARTY subject to PHIPA. DISCLOSING PARTY is, as defined in PHIPA, a health information custodian and/or agent and has statutory obligations to safeguard its patients PHI.
flags.rationale: The clause addresses statutory obligations under PHIPA (Personal Health Information Protection Act), which is specific to Ontario, Canada. This topic is not covered by the UoA Preferred Contracting Positions or any registered UoA template for this contract type. As such, it falls outside the scope of the UoA policy framework and is flagged as blue.

“health information custodian” and/or “agent” and has statutory obligations to safeguard its patients’ PHI.

flags.clause_title: 3. Receiving Party May Obtain Access to PHI. It is anticipated that Receiving
flags.snippet: 3. Receiving Party May Obtain Access to PHI. It is anticipated that Receiving Party may require access to PHI from the DISCLOSING PARTY in the course of fulfilling its obligations under this Agreement. If the Receiving Party does obtain access to PHI while fulfilling the Agreement, the provisions set in this Schedule “B” set out the obligations of the Receiving Party with respect to its collection, use, disclosure, retention and disposal of such PHI.
flags.rationale: The clause addresses access to Protected Health Information (PHI), which is not covered by the UoA Preferred Contracting Positions or any registered UoA Standard Template for this contract type. As such, it falls outside the scope of the documented positions and requires independent review.

flags.clause_title: 4. Receiving Party obligations under PHIPA. The parties acknowledge and agree
flags.snippet: 4. Receiving Party obligations under PHIPA. The parties acknowledge and agree that Receiving Party, when accessing PHI, and where applicable, does so solely with the explicit permission from the DISCLOSING PARTY’s Study Participants to enable the DISCLOSING PARTY to fulfill its obligations under this Agreement. As such, the Receiving Party is a health information agent and is thus subject to all provisions of and obligations under PHIPA.
flags.rationale: The clause addresses obligations under PHIPA (Personal Health Information Protection Act), which is specific to health information and privacy regulations. This topic is not covered by the UoA Preferred Contracting Positions or any registered UoA template for this contract type. As such, it falls outside the scope of the existing policy framework and requires independent assessment.

DISCLOSING PARTY’s Study Participants to enable the DISCLOSING PARTY to fulfill its obligations
under this Agreement. As such, the Receiving Party is a health information agent and is thus subject to all
provisions of and obligations under PHIPA.

6. Obligations not Conditional. The Receiving Party’s obligations under this Schedule “B” stem
from the obligations of both the DISCLOSING PARTY and the Receiving Party under PHIPA, and in
light of the foregoing, are not conditional on the DISCLOSING PARTY’s compliance with any of its
obligations under this Agreement, including any obligation to pay the Receiving Party, provided that for
greater certainty these obligations shall only apply to the extent that the Receiving Party has custody or
control of personal information or PHI under this Agreement.

that it has provided training to its Personnel with respect to Receiving Party’s legal obligations respecting

flags.clause_title: 11. Compliance with Privacy Legislation. Receiving Party represents and warran
flags.snippet: 11. Compliance with Privacy Legislation. Receiving Party represents and warrants to the DISCLOSING PARTY that it: a. has a privacy policy in compliance with applicable privacy legislation, addressing its practices relat…
flags.rationale: The clause addresses compliance with privacy legislation, including privacy policies, safeguards, and the appointment of a privacy compliance officer. This topic is not covered by the UoA Preferred Contracting Positions or any registered UoA Standard Template for this contract type. As such, it falls outside the scope of pre-defined positions and is flagged as blue.
flags.text:
11. Compliance with Privacy Legislation. Receiving Party represents and warrants to the
DISCLOSING PARTY that it:
a. has a privacy policy in compliance with applicable privacy legislation, addressing its practices
relating to the collection, use, disclosure, retention and disposal of personal information;
b. shall monitor and enforce compliance with its own privacy policy;
c. has an appointed privacy compliance officer who shall be given the responsibility for Receiving
Party’s compliance with the terms and conditions under this Agreement; and
d. has implemented effective administrative, technological and physical safeguards to stop theft,
loss and unauthorized access, copying, modification, use, disclosure or disposal of personal
information and that such safeguards are consistent with industry practice.

flags.clause_title: 14. Theft, Loss or Unauthorized Access of PHI. In the event that the Receiving
flags.snippet: 14. Theft, Loss or Unauthorized Access of PHI. In the event that the Receiving Party becomes aware that PHI has been stolen or lost, or a person has obtained unauthorized access to PHI, or Receiving Party has used, disclosed or disposed of the PHI other than as contemplated in this Agreement, Receiving Party shall promptly notify the DISCLOSING PARTY’s contact as stipulated under the Agreement, and shall not otherwise disclose to any third party such circumstances without the prior written consent of the DISCLOSING PARTY except as required by law.
flags.rationale: The clause addresses the handling of theft, loss, or unauthorized access of PHI (Protected Health Information), which is not covered by any UoA Preferred Contracting Positions or the UoA Standard Template for this contract type. As such, it falls outside the scope of the documented positions and is flagged as blue.
flags.text:
14. Theft, Loss or Unauthorized Access of PHI. In the event that the Receiving Party becomes
aware that PHI has been stolen or lost, or a person has obtained unauthorized access to PHI, or Receiving
Party has used, disclosed or disposed of the PHI other than as contemplated in this Agreement, Receiving
Party shall promptly notify the DISCLOSING PARTY’s contact as stipulated under the Agreement, and
shall not otherwise disclose to any third party such circumstances without the prior written consent of the
DISCLOSING PARTY except as required by law.

flags.snippet: 15. DISCLOSING PARTY’s Review of Receiving Party’s Practices and Procedures. The DISCLOSING PARTY may, upon reasonable notice, assess and review Receiving Party’s practices and procedures for receiving and processing PHI under this Agreement, for the purposes of ensuring that the terms and conditions are being complied with. For these purposes, upon request Receiving Party shall provide the DISCLOSING PARTY with reasonable access to the policies, procedures and protocols used for purposes of fulfilling its obligations under this Agreement and any other documents that may be relevant.
flags.rationale: The clause addresses the DISCLOSING PARTY's right to review the Receiving Party's practices and procedures for handling PHI (Protected Health Information). This topic is not covered in the UoA Preferred Contracting Positions document, nor is there a standard template for this contract type. As such, the clause falls outside the scope of pre-defined UoA positions and is flagged as blue.

15. DISCLOSING PARTY’s Review of Receiving Party’s Practices and Procedures. The
DISCLOSING PARTY may, upon reasonable notice, assess and review Receiving Party’s practices and
procedures for receiving and processing PHI under this Agreement, for the purposes of ensuring that the
terms and conditions are being complied with. For these purposes, upon request Receiving Party shall
provide the DISCLOSING PARTY with reasonable access to the policies, procedures and protocols used
for purposes of fulfilling its obligations under this Agreement and any other documents that may be
relevant.

flags.clause_title: 17. Complaint by the DISCLOSING PARTY. In the event that the DISCLOSING PARTY
flags.snippet: 17. Complaint by the DISCLOSING PARTY. In the event that the DISCLOSING PARTY makes a complaint to Receiving Party in respect of Receiving Partys compliance with this Agreement, the Receiving Party shall, within a comm
flags.rationale: The clause addresses a complaint mechanism related to compliance with the agreement, which is not covered by any UoA Preferred Contracting Positions or standard template. As such, it falls outside the scope of pre-defined UoA policies and requires independent assessment.
flags.text:
17. Complaint by the DISCLOSING PARTY. In the event that the DISCLOSING PARTY makes a
complaint to Receiving Party in respect of Receiving Party’s compliance with this Agreement, the
Receiving Party shall, within a commercially reasonable period of time from receipt of the complaint,
investigate the matter and provide the DISCLOSING PARTY with an oral report stating the cause of the
deficiency, if any, and the steps taken to prevent a recurrence, if required. Within a further five business
days, Receiving Party shall provide the DISCLOSING PARTY with a written report documenting the
complaint, investigation, deficiency, if any, and the steps taken to prevent a recurrence, if required.
9

d) Entire Agreement: This Agreement including the attached Schedule(s) represents the entire Agreement
between or among the Parties related to the Study and supersedes all previously or contemporaneously
executed agreements related to the Study. If there are any conflicts between the terms of the Protocol and
the terms of this Agreement, the terms of the Protocol will govern with respect to scientific and medical
matters, and the terms of this Agreement will govern with respect to all other matters.

flags.clause_title: j) Force Majeure Clause: Noncompliance by a party with the obligations of this
flags.snippet: j) Force Majeure Clause: Noncompliance by a party with the obligations of this Agreement due to force majeure, including laws or regulations of any government, war, civil commotion, destruction of production facilities …

flags.standard_ref: Not covered by UoA Position or Template
flags.text:
j) Force Majeure Clause: Noncompliance by a party with the obligations of this Agreement due to force
majeure, including laws or regulations of any government, war, civil commotion, destruction of production
facilities and materials, fire, flood, earthquake or storm, labor disturbances, shortage of materials, disease,
epidemic, public health crisis, failure of public utilities or common carriers, or any other causes beyond the
reasonable control of the applicable party, shall not constitute breach of this Agreement and such party shall
be excused from performance hereunder to the extent and for the duration of such prevention, provided it
first notifies the other Parties in writing of such prevention and that it uses reasonable efforts to cause the
event of the force majeure to terminate, be cured or otherwise ended.

k) Counterparts: This Agreement may be executed by way of electronic signature and in any number of
counterparts with the same effect as if all Parties had signed the same document. All of these counterparts
will for all purposes constitute one Agreement, binding on the Parties, notwithstanding that all Parties are
not signatories to the same counterpart. A faxed or emailed PDF copy or photocopy of this Agreement
executed by a party in counterpart or otherwise will constitute a properly executed, delivered and binding
agreement or counterpart of the executing party.

flags.clause_title: DATA TRANSFER AGREEMENT
flags.snippet: DATA TRANSFER AGREEMENT This Agreement is entered into between the Parties to govern the mutual transfer of the Data from Disclosing Party to Receiving Party. This Agreement is made by and between: University of Aucklan…
flags.rationale: The clause addresses the topic of data transfer agreements, which is not covered by the UoA Preferred Contracting Positions or any registered UoA Standard Template for this contract type. As such, it falls outside the scope of the defined policy tiers and is flagged as blue.
flags.text:
DATA TRANSFER AGREEMENT

This Agreement is entered into between the Parties to govern the mutual transfer of the Data from Disclosing Party to Receiving Party.

This Agreement is made by and between:

University of Auckland, through its AUCKLAND BIOENGINEERING INSTITUTE (“Collaborator”) with address at: 22 Princes Street, Auckland, New Zealand

Contact information for administration:
[REDACTED]
Physical Address: 22 Princes Street, Auckland 1010, New Zealand
Postal Address: Private Bag 92019, Auckland 1142, New Zealand
Email: contracts@auckland.ac.nz

and

[REDACTED] with an address at 22 Princes Street, Auckland 1010, New Zealand (“Collaborating Investigator”)

and

[REDACTED] (“[REDACTED]”)

Contact information for administration
[REDACTED]
[REDACTED]
[REDACTED]

and

[REDACTED], who holds an appointment at [REDACTED] with an address at [REDACTED] (“[REDACTED] Investigator")

This Agreement is made pursuant to sections 44(5) and 44(6) of PHIPA.

[REDACTED]

WHEREAS, the Parties wish to transfer Data for the purposes of the Study;

NOW THEREFORE, in consideration of the mutual covenants and agreements exchanged between the Parties and other good and valuable consideration, the receipt and sufficiency of which are hereby acknowledged, the Parties agree as follows:

flags.clause_title: SCHEDULE “B”
flags.snippet: SCHEDULE “B” PRIVACY ADDENDUM FOR TRANSFER OF PHI
flags.rationale: The clause addresses privacy and the transfer of PHI (Protected Health Information), which is not covered by the UoA Preferred Contracting Positions or any registered UoA template for this contract type. As such, it falls outside the scope of the provided policy framework.
flags.text:
SCHEDULE “B”
PRIVACY ADDENDUM FOR TRANSFER OF PHI

flags.clause_title: 9. Obligations/Liability: Each party shall be responsible and assume liability
flags.snippet: 9. Obligations/Liability: Each party shall be responsible and assume liability for any damage or loss to the extent that such damage or loss arises out of or as a result of its own negligence or willful misconduct or th…
flags.rationale: The clause aligns with UoA Position #POS-07 on Liability Limitations and Exclusions. It limits liability to direct losses caused by negligence or willful misconduct and explicitly excludes indirect or consequential damages, including lost profits and opportunities. This matches the Preferred tier of the UoA Position. The clause also includes a requirement for reasonable insurance coverage, which is consistent with UoA's expectations.
flags.text:
9. Obligations/Liability: Each party shall be responsible and assume liability for any damage or loss to the
extent that such damage or loss arises out of or as a result of its own negligence or willful misconduct or the
negligence or willful misconduct of those for whom in law it/he/she is responsible. No Party or its trustees,
directors, officers, employees, and agents (the “First Party”) shall be liable to any other Party (the “Second
Party”) for any costs, suits, or claims made by the Second Party or made against the Second Party except to
the extent caused by negligence or willful misconduct on the part of the First Party. The Parties shall
maintain reasonable insurance coverage for such liabilities. No Party shall be responsible for any lost profits,
lost opportunities, or other indirect or consequential damages suffered by another Party.

flags.clause_title: b) No Warranty: Data is provided on an “as-is” basis and Disclosing Party make
flags.snippet: b) No Warranty: Data is provided on an “as-is” basis and Disclosing Party makes no representations or warranties, express or implied, with respect thereto. Receiving Party accepts that there are no representations, warranties, conditions or liabilities expressed or implied herewith in relation to the Data by Disclosing Party or their respective trustees, directors, officers, affiliates, investigators, students, employees, servants, authorized representatives or agents.
flags.rationale: The clause aligns with UoA Position #POS-10 on Warranties, which requires the exclusion of all warranties beyond authority to contract and professional performance. The clause explicitly excludes all representations, warranties, conditions, and liabilities, which matches the Preferred tier. No escalation is required.
flags.text:
b) No Warranty: Data is provided on an “as-is” basis and Disclosing Party makes no representations or
warranties, express or implied, with respect thereto. Receiving Party accepts that there are no
representations, warranties, conditions or liabilities expressed or implied herewith in relation to the Data by
Disclosing Party or their respective trustees, directors, officers, affiliates, investigators, students, employees,
servants, authorized representatives or agents.

flags.clause_title: h) Use of Name/Symbols: No party shall use, or authorize others to use, the na
flags.snippet: h) Use of Name/Symbols: No party shall use, or authorize others to use, the name, symbols, or marks of another party hereto or its staff for any endorsement purposes without prior written approval from the party whose n…
flags.rationale: The clause aligns with UoA Position #POS-14 on Publicity, which states that neither party can use names or brands of the other or their personnel without prior written consent. The clause explicitly requires prior written approval for the use of names, symbols, or marks, which matches the Preferred tier.
flags.text:
h) Use of Name/Symbols: No party shall use, or authorize others to use, the name, symbols, or marks of
another party hereto or its staff for any endorsement purposes without prior written approval from the party
whose name, symbols or marks are to be used.
